 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8.2","title":"Simulate: evaluation loop + aggregation (counts, top rules)","description":"## Objective

Implement the core simulation loop that:

- evaluates each parsed command with the shared evaluator
- aggregates results into actionable summaries

## Aggregations (v1)

- totals: allow / warn / deny
- per-rule counts (`rule_id`)
- per-pack counts (`pack_id`)
- exemplar sampling per rule (keep top K shortest / first K occurrences)

## Determinism

- Sorting of summary outputs must be deterministic (count desc then rule_id asc).
- Sampling must be deterministic (first K occurrences by file order).

## Acceptance criteria

- `dcg simulate` produces stable output given the same input file.
- Aggregation respects limits (`--limit`, `--top N`, etc.).

## Dependencies

- `git_safety_guard-1gt.8.1` (parser)
- `git_safety_guard-99e.3.1` (shared evaluator)
","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### Tests
- Aggregation output must be deterministic:
  - stable sorting for “top rules” and “top packs”
  - stable bucketing for allow/warn/deny
- Unit tests should assert stable ordering and correct counts.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:15:42.58033159-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T23:29:46.96506937-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:15:52.76100775-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:15:57.931365713-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8.3","title":"Simulate: output formats (pretty/json) + redaction/truncation","description":"## Objective

Implement `dcg simulate` output formatting in both human and machine forms.

## Pretty output requirements

- Summary header (totals + elapsed time)
- Top rules table:
  - rule_id
  - count
  - decision (deny/warn)
  - 1-line reason (if available)
- Optional section: sample commands per top rule (truncated + redacted)

## JSON output requirements

- `schema_version`
- totals
- `rules[]`: `{ rule_id, pack_id, decision, count, reason?, examples[] }`
- `errors[]`: parse errors counts, malformed lines count

## Privacy

- Default to truncation in examples.
- Support `--redact` consistent with scan/explain.

## Acceptance criteria

- Output is stable for golden tests.
- JSON output is valid and additive-only for schema v1.

## Dependencies

- `git_safety_guard-1gt.8.2` (aggregation)
","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### Determinism
- Output formats must be deterministic and stable for diffing:
  - stable JSON schema versioning
  - stable sorting of aggregated tables
  - stable truncation/redaction rules","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:16:19.160687587-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T23:30:01.561272192-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:16:29.475130683-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:16:34.556618726-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8.4","title":"Simulate: fixtures + integration tests + detailed logging","description":"## Objective

Add tests that make `dcg simulate` trustworthy and easy to evolve.

## Test strategy

### Unit tests

- parser unit tests (format detection) live with `git_safety_guard-1gt.8.1`.

### Integration tests (this task)

- Run the compiled binary with `dcg simulate` against fixture log files.
- Assert:
  - exit codes
  - key summary counts
  - top rule ordering stability
  - redaction/truncation behavior

## Logging requirements

Test failures should print:

- which fixture file
- which line number caused parse failure
- the offending line (truncated)
- the expected vs actual summary

## Acceptance criteria

- Tests are deterministic across runs.
- Adding a new fixture regression is easy.

## Dependencies

- `git_safety_guard-1gt.8.3` (output)

+{"id":"git_safety_guard-1uf","title":"E2E: path normalization + quoting/encoding matrix","description":"## Objective

Expand E2E coverage around tricky path/quoting scenarios that historically cause false positives/negatives.

## Scope

- Absolute paths (`/usr/bin/git`, `/bin/rm`) plus quoted command words.
- Mixed quoting, escaped characters, Unicode edge cases, env assignments, and sudo/env wrappers.
- Encode tests for command substitution and pipeline contexts.

## Acceptance criteria

- New matrix cases added to E2E suite with clear expected results.
- Failures are easy to diagnose from logs.
","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-08T23:31:06.365458279-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T23:31:06.365458279-05:00"}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-26f","title":"Define TypeScript destructive patterns for heredoc scanning","description":"## Objective

Define TypeScript heredoc patterns with a focus on **runtime destructive operations**, while avoiding low-signal “TypeScript style” heuristics that would create false positives.

## Scope boundary

TypeScript compiles to JavaScript; destructive operations are the same at runtime.

Therefore:

- Reuse the JavaScript/Node pattern families from `git_safety_guard-mcf`.
- Add only TypeScript-syntax coverage (types, generics, decorators) so the parser can still match the same runtime calls.

## Pattern families (v1)

- Recursive filesystem deletion:
  - `fs.rmSync(..., { recursive: true })`
  - `fs.rmdirSync(..., { recursive: true })`
- Process execution with destructive payload:
  - `child_process.execSync("rm -rf ...")`
  - `spawnSync("rm", ["-rf", ...])`

## Explicitly out of scope (avoid FPs)

- Heuristics like “@ts-ignore preceding dangerous code”
- “any type casts” / “type assertions” as signals

These are too common and do not map directly to destructive operations.

## Acceptance Criteria

- TS patterns are a superset of JS patterns in terms of runtime destructive operations, differing only in syntax support.
- Includes positive + negative fixtures that exercise TS syntax (types/generics/decorators) without changing runtime behavior.
- No default hard denies unless the matched operation is clearly catastrophic.
","notes":"Taking this next to unblock git_safety_guard-e7m: expand TypeScript heredoc patterns (mirror JS families) + add TS-syntax-focused fixtures; reuse/refine catastrophic-path and destructive-literal payload detection; run full gates + push.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonFalcon","created_at":"2026-01-07T18:37:17.541114003-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T06:09:08.474522952-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T06:09:08.474522952-05:00","close_reason":"Expanded TypeScript heredoc patterns to mirror JS families (fs rm/rmdir + execSync/spawnSync + Deno.remove), with refinement to block only on catastrophic literal paths and destructive literal shell payloads. Added ≥5 positive + ≥5 negative TS fixtures covering TS syntax (type assertions, generics, decorators). Full fmt/check/clippy/test + e2e pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-26f","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6sg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T18:37:34.25492655-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-26f","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-f4f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T18:37:34.273731104-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-26f","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-mcf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T18:37:34.292923988-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-26f","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-yza","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:50:44.680006442-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-2cu","title":"Design false positive reduction strategy for string arguments","description":"## Objective

Design and implement strategies to dramatically reduce false positives in command blocking, especially for documentation and string arguments.

## The Core Problem

The current regex-based guard suffers from context blindness. It sees:
```
bd create --description="Pattern blocks rm -rf commands"
```

And matches "rm -rf" without understanding it's:
1. Inside a quoted string
2. An argument to a documentation tool
3. NOT actually being executed

This creates severe workflow disruption when trying to document the very patterns we're blocking.

## Why This Is Critical

False positives are arguably WORSE than false negatives:
- False negative: A dangerous command might slip through (rare, recoverable with backups)
- False positive: Blocks legitimate work, forces workarounds, erodes trust in the tool
- Repeated false positives lead users to disable the guard entirely

The guard must be TRUSTED to make intelligent decisions, not blindly pattern-match text.

## Solution Approaches

### 1. Command Structure Analysis (Primary Approach)

Parse the top-level command with a lightweight shell/token model to understand structure:
- Identify command name (bd, git, echo, etc.)
- Identify argument positions (which are options vs values)
- Identify quoting context (single, double, unquoted)
- Only apply destructive patterns to EXECUTABLE positions

Example analysis:
```
bd create --title="..." --description="rm -rf pattern docs"
         ^command       ^option         ^string value (NOT executed)
```

### 2. Safe Command Registry

Maintain a list of commands that take non-executable string arguments:
- bd create, bd update (--description, --title)
- git commit (-m), git tag (-m)
- echo, printf (arguments are printed, not executed)
- grep, rg (pattern arguments)

For these commands, don't apply destructive patterns to their string arguments.

### 3. Execution Context Detection

Distinguish between:
- **Direct execution**: The string IS the command (`bash dangerous_cmd`)
- **String literal**: The string is DATA passed to a command (`bd --desc="..."`)
- **Heredoc body**: Requires language-specific analysis (the whole point of ast-grep)

### 4. Two-Phase Analysis

1. **Quick structural check**: parse command structure, identify context
2. **Pattern matching**: only apply patterns to executable contexts
3. **Deep analysis**: for heredocs/complex cases, use ast-grep / language-aware scanning

### 5. Confidence Scoring (optional later)

Instead of binary block/allow:
- High confidence dangerous: block immediately
- Medium confidence: more thorough analysis
- Low confidence (looks like documentation): allow with optional warning

## Implementation Strategy

1. Choose the minimal parsing substrate that meets performance needs:
   - tokenization + quoting + operator detection (pipes/subst)
   - optionally tree-sitter-bash if needed for correctness
2. Define `ExecutionContext` enum: Direct, StringArg, Heredoc, PipeTarget, CommandSubst
3. Maintain a Safe String-Argument Registry (command + flags) and apply it only in `StringArg` context.
4. Ensure ambiguity defaults to safe behavior (do not “trust” unknown flags as safe).

## Test Cases

Essential false positive tests:
- `bd create --description="This blocks rm -rf"` → ALLOW
- `git commit -m "Fix rm -rf pattern matching"` → ALLOW
- `echo "example: git reset --hard"` → ALLOW
- `grep "rm -rf" patterns.txt` → ALLOW

Essential true positive tests (should still block):
- `rm -rf /tmp/*` → BLOCK
- `bash -c "rm -rf /"` → BLOCK
- `python3 << 'EOF'
  import os; os.system("rm -rf /")
  EOF` → BLOCK (via heredoc analysis)

## Acceptance Criteria

- Design is captured in a self-contained doc/ADR-like section (does not require reading external plan docs).
- Defines:
  - the execution-context model
  - the initial Safe String-Argument Registry scope
  - conservative default rules for ambiguity
- Provides a concrete test matrix of:
  - must-allow doc/test cases
  - must-block executed cases
- Specifies a performance budget for the parsing/classification layer (initial target: sub-5ms worst-case, revise after baseline).

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-2d4","title":"Define Perl destructive patterns for heredoc scanning","description":"## Objective

Define Perl heredoc matchers that detect **high-signal destructive operations** while minimizing false positives.

## Scope boundary

- Focus on irreversible filesystem/process operations.
- Avoid broad “Perl is scary” patterns (eval/regex/taint/network) as default denies; they are not directly destructive and will produce noise.

## Pattern families (v1)

### 1) Process execution with destructive payload

- `system("rm -rf ...")` (literal)
- backticks / `qx//` containing literal destructive payload
- `exec("git reset --hard ...")` (literal)

### 2) Recursive deletion / filesystem destructive ops

- `File::Path::rmtree($path)` (literal catastrophic target → Error; otherwise Warning)
- `unlink($path)` / `rmdir($path)` (Warning by default unless catastrophic target)

## False positive mitigation

- Do not flag `system($VAR)` as deny.
- Do not flag module imports alone.
- Require negative fixtures that include documentation strings and harmless cleanup.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Defines a Perl pattern set aligned with severity taxonomy and focused on destructive outcomes.
- Includes at least 5 positive and 5 negative fixtures.
- No default hard denies unless the matched operation is clearly catastrophic.
","notes":"CrimsonFalcon: implementing Perl heredoc matcher fallback (regex/heuristic) + unit tests; will run full quality gates and push.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T18:37:19.324205765-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T05:27:04.470945278-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T05:27:04.470945278-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented Perl regex fallback matcher + unit fixtures; fixed normalization idempotency regression uncovered by proptest","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-2d4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6sg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T18:37:37.366046506-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-2d4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-f4f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T18:37:37.385299864-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-2d4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-yza","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:50:49.808754149-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-e7m","title":"Integrate heredoc detection into main check pipeline","description":"## Objective

Integrate all heredoc detection components into the main dcg check pipeline, creating a seamless flow from command input to block/allow decision.

## Pipeline Architecture

```
JSON Input → Parse → Quick Reject → Normalize →
  → Heredoc Detection → [if heredoc found] →
    → Extract Content → Detect Language → Parse with ast-grep →
    → Apply Language Patterns → Block Decision
  → [no heredoc] → Existing Pattern Matching → Block Decision
```

## Integration Points

### 1. Entry Point Hook

Modify main.rs check flow to:

1. After quick reject passes, check for heredoc indicators
2. If heredoc found, branch to heredoc analysis path
3. If no heredoc, continue with existing pattern matching
4. Merge results from both paths

### 2. Heredoc Detection Integration

Insert heredoc detection before main pattern matching:

```rust
fn check_command(cmd: &str) -> CheckResult {
    // Quick reject (existing)
    if global_quick_reject(cmd) {
        return CheckResult::allowed();
    }

    // NEW: Heredoc detection
    if let Some(heredoc_result) = check_heredoc(cmd) {
        if heredoc_result.blocked {
            return heredoc_result;
        }
    }

    // Existing pattern matching
    REGISTRY.check_command(cmd, &enabled_packs)
}
```

### 3. Performance Budget

Total additional latency budget must be bounded.

- Tier 1 trigger: microseconds
- Tier 2 extraction: ~1ms typical
- Tier 3 AST/pattern: bounded by timeout (hard cap)

If AST analysis is too slow, fail-open (allow) in hook mode and emit a diagnostic.

### 4. Feature Flag / Pack integration

Add heredoc scanning to pack system:

- New pack: "heredoc" (or integrate into core)
- Can be enabled/disabled via configuration
- Default policy should be conservative (warn-first / strict mode opt-in)

### 5. Error Handling

Graceful degradation:

- AST engine unavailable → allow (hook) + diagnostic; optional fallback to simpler scanning
- AST timeout → allow (hook) + diagnostic
- Parse error → allow (hook) + diagnostic
- Unknown language → allow (hook) or generic high-signal checks only

## Configuration

New config options:

```toml
[heredoc]
enabled = true
timeout_ms = 50
fallback_on_error = true
languages = ["python", "bash", "javascript", "typescript", "ruby", "perl"]
```

## Testing Integration

Integration tests should cover:

- Normal commands (no heredoc) still work fast
- Heredoc commands get analyzed
- Pattern matches in heredocs block correctly
- Safe heredocs pass through
- Error paths handle gracefully

## Acceptance Criteria

- Heredoc path is executed only when Tier 1 triggers; non-heredoc commands remain fast.
- In hook mode, any heredoc parsing timeout/error fails open (allow) with a trace/log marker.
- At least one E2E test demonstrates a real heredoc block (direct destructive payload).
- At least one E2E test demonstrates a false-positive suppression case (dangerous string in data-only context).
- Decisions are attributed with stable rule IDs (pack_id + pattern_name) so explain/allowlist workflows are possible.
","notes":"Claiming to integrate heredoc detection/extraction/AST matching into main check pipeline (hook + CLI parity).  {"id":"git_safety_guard-ehc","title":"Implement GitLab CI extractor (.gitlab-ci.yml)","description":"## Objective

Implement an extractor for GitLab CI YAML files to detect executable command contexts.

## File Pattern

- `.gitlab-ci.yml`
- `*.gitlab-ci.yml` (includes)

## Extraction Targets

### Script Blocks (executed)
- `script:` arrays
- `before_script:` arrays
- `after_script:` arrays

### NOT Extracted (data only)
- `variables:` values
- `rules:` conditions
- `only:`/`except:` patterns
- Comments

## Example

```yaml
deploy:
  script:
    - kubectl delete namespace prod  # EXTRACT THIS
  variables:
    DANGEROUS: "rm -rf /"  # DO NOT extract
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- Correctly extracts script blocks with line numbers
- Ignores variable definitions and comments
- Handles YAML anchors and aliases
- Unit tests with representative fixtures","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### False positive controls
- Extract only from executed job script contexts (e.g., `script:` sections), not from:
  - variables
  - comments
  - descriptive fields
- Include negative fixtures covering “dangerous substring in docs/variables” cases.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:24:57.250174884-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T23:30:21.225078074-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ehc","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:25:20.517428749-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ehc","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:46:22.595864802-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-erux","title":"Design user-friendly error messages for heredoc blocks","description":"# User-Friendly Error Messages for Heredoc Blocking

## Objective

When a heredoc command is blocked, provide clear, actionable error messages that help users understand WHY it was blocked and HOW to proceed.

## Current Error Format (for reference)

```
╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║  ⚠️  BLOCKED: Destructive Command Detected                   ║
╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
║  Command: git reset --hard HEAD~5                            ║
║  Reason:  Hard reset can permanently lose commits            ║
║  Pack:    core.git                                           ║
╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
```

## Enhanced Error Format for Heredocs

```
╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║  ⚠️  BLOCKED: Destructive Pattern in Heredoc                 ║
╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
║  Language: Python                                            ║
║  Pattern:  os.system($CMD)                                   ║
║  Matched:  os.system("rm -rf /tmp/data")                    ║
║  Line:     3 of heredoc                                      ║
║  Reason:   Shell command execution via os.system             ║
║  Pack:     heredoc.python                                    ║
╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
║  Context:                                                    ║
║    1│ import os                                              ║
║    2│ path = "/tmp/data"                                    ║
║  ▶ 3│ os.system("rm -rf /tmp/data")  ← BLOCKED             ║
║    4│ print("done")                                         ║
╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
║  💡 Suggestions:                                             ║
║  • Use pathlib.Path.unlink() for single file deletion       ║
║  • Use shutil.rmtree() with explicit path validation        ║
║  • If intentional, use dcg allow --reason "..."             ║
╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
```

## Error Message Components

### 1. Header

- Clear indication this is a BLOCK, not just a warning
- Specify it's a heredoc/inline script detection

### 2. Detection Details

- Language detected
- Pattern name that matched
- Actual matched text (truncated if long)
- Line number within heredoc
- Human-readable reason

### 3. Context Window

- Show 2-3 lines before and after the match
- Highlight the offending line with arrow
- Syntax highlighting if terminal supports it

### 4. Suggestions

- Language-specific safe alternatives
- How to override if intentional
- Link to documentation

## JSON Output Format

For Claude Code hook protocol:

```json
{
  "hookSpecificOutput": {
    "permissionDecision": "deny",
    "reason": "Heredoc contains destructive pattern: os.system with shell command",
    "details": {
      "detection_type": "heredoc",
      "language": "python",
      "pattern": "os.system($CMD)",
      "matched_text": "os.system(\"rm -rf /tmp/data\")",
      "line_in_heredoc": 3,
      "pack": "heredoc.python",
      "suggestions": [
        "Use pathlib.Path.unlink() for single file deletion",
        "Use shutil.rmtree() with explicit path validation"
      ]
    }
  }
}
```

## Suggestions Database

Maintain per-pattern suggestions:

```rust
static PATTERN_SUGGESTIONS: &[(&str, &[&str])] = &[
    ("os.system", &[
        "Use subprocess.run() with shell=False",
        "Use pathlib for file operations",
    ]),
    ("shutil.rmtree", &[
        "Validate path is within expected directory",
        "Use explicit allowlist of deletable paths",
    ]),
];
```

## Test Cases

- Verify all pattern types have suggestions
- Verify context window is correctly extracted
- Verify line numbers are accurate
- Verify JSON output is valid
- Verify color output is readable
- Verify truncation for long matches

## Acceptance Criteria

- When heredoc detection blocks, stderr output includes:
  - language
  - stable rule ID (pack_id:pattern_name)
  - a small context window with highlighted line
  - actionable next steps (explain/allowlist)
- Hook JSON denial remains valid and includes enough structured details for tooling.
- Output is safe for non-TTY environments (no ANSI when not a TTY).
- Unit tests cover at least:
  - context window extraction
  - truncation
  - JSON schema validity

Improve E2E scripts to produce **actionable, structured logs** and timing data for failures.

## Scope

- Add per-test timing and aggregate timing summary.
- Optional JSON log output (machine-readable), alongside current human-friendly logs.
- Capture stdout/stderr on failure and store as artifacts in CI.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-f4f","title":"Implement ast-grep-core pattern matching layer","description":"# ast-grep-core Pattern Matching Layer

## Objective

Integrate ast-grep-core as a Rust library for structural pattern matching in heredoc/inline-script content.

## Architecture decision

Prefer **embedded ast-grep-core** over shelling out to a CLI, because:

- avoids per-invocation process spawn overhead
- keeps a single-binary distribution story
- enables richer match metadata (spans) for `dcg explain`

(Still subject to ADR confirmation; this task implements whichever embedded approach is chosen.)

## Dependencies to add (tentative)

```toml
[dependencies]
ast-grep-core = "0.40"
ast-grep-language = { version = "0.37", features = [
    "bash",
    "python",
    "javascript",
    "typescript",
    "ruby"
    # Note: Perl may not be available
] }
```

## API design

```rust
/// Pattern matcher using ast-grep-core
pub struct AstMatcher {
    patterns: HashMap<Language, Vec<CompiledPattern>>,
}

impl AstMatcher {
    /// Create matcher with default destructive patterns
    pub fn new() -> Self;

    /// Load patterns from config / registry
    pub fn from_config(config: &PatternConfig) -> Result<Self>;

    /// Match patterns against code (bounded)
    pub fn find_matches(&self, code: &str, language: Language) -> Result<Vec<PatternMatch>, MatchError>;
}

/// Result of pattern matching
pub struct PatternMatch {
    pub rule_id: String,             // stable: pack_id:pattern_name
    pub reason: String,
    pub matched_text_preview: String,
    pub start: usize,                // byte offset (or row/col)
    pub end: usize,
    pub line_number: usize,
    pub severity: Severity,          // ties into decision policy
}

pub enum Severity { Error, Warning, Info }
```

## Pattern definition format (example)

Patterns should be defined and reviewed under `git_safety_guard-6sg` (pattern library structure). The matching layer must be agnostic to *what* the patterns are, but must support:

- stable IDs
- per-pattern severity
- per-pattern suggestions (optional)

## Error handling / fail-open

In hook mode:

- Unsupported language → skip analysis (allow) + trace marker
- Parse error → skip analysis (allow) + trace marker
- Timeout → skip analysis (allow) + trace marker

In `dcg test` / `dcg explain` / `dcg scan`:

- return structured diagnostics (not silent skip)

## Performance targets (initial)

- Pattern compilation: one-time at startup (bounded)
- Parse: <2ms for typical heredoc sizes
- Match: <1ms typical
- Total AST path: bounded by a hard timeout

## Acceptance Criteria

- Implements an embedded AST matcher that can:
  - parse at least one supported language
  - match at least one simple pattern
  - return match spans + line number
- Enforces a hard timeout for AST work and fails open in hook mode on timeout.
- Produces match metadata compatible with:
  - `dcg explain` highlighting (matched span + preview)
  - rule attribution (stable rule_id)
  - decision policy (severity)
- Includes unit tests for:
  - a positive match
  - a negative (safe) snippet
  - an unsupported-language path
  - a timeout path (if testable)
","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### False positive control
This layer must support the FP-immunity strategy, not undermine it:

- matchers should attach stable rule IDs + severity so policy can default-warn vs deny
- matchers should support negative fixtures / “must-not-match” tests per rule family
- prefer composite/high-signal rules (AST match + literal destructive payload) over broad matches (e.g., `exec($VAR)`)

### Tests
- For each supported language: at least one positive and one negative snippet.
- Span/line mapping test: matches return accurate location for explain output.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T18:32:24.313325332-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T02:41:21.25882879-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T02:41:21.25882879-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented ast-grep-core pattern matching layer with AstMatcher struct, find_matches(), timeout handling, and 17 unit tests. All acceptance criteria met.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-f4f","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-5ib","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T18:32:32.909572665-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-f4f","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-du4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T18:32:32.929896409-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-f4f","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-yza","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:50:18.965756128-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-fpim","title":"Implement false positive reduction for string arguments","description":"# False Positive Reduction Implementation

## Problem

Current regex patterns match dangerous strings even in safe contexts:
- `bd create --description="This blocks rm -rf"` → BLOCKED (wrong!)
- `git commit -m "Fix rm -rf pattern"` → BLOCKED (wrong!)
- `grep "rm -rf" patterns.txt` → BLOCKED (wrong!)

## Solution

Parse command structure to identify execution context before applying destructive patterns.

## Implementation

### 1. Safe Command Registry

```rust
static SAFE_STRING_ARG_COMMANDS: &[(&str, &[&str])] = &[
    ("bd", &["--description", "--title", "-d", "-t"]),
    ("git", &["commit -m", "tag -m", "notes add -m"]),
    ("grep", &["-e", "--regexp"]),
    ("rg", &["-e", "--regexp"]),
    ("echo", &[]),  // all args are strings
    ("printf", &[]),
];
```

### 2. Execution Context Detection

```rust
enum ExecutionContext {
    Direct,           // Command itself is executed
    StringArgument,   // Inside quoted string argument to safe command
    HeredocBody,      // Inside heredoc (needs Tier 2 analysis)
    PipeTarget,       // Right side of pipe (executed)
    CommandSubst,     // Inside $() or backticks (executed)
}

fn detect_execution_context(cmd: &str, position: usize) -> ExecutionContext;
```

### 3. Integration Point

Modify main check flow:

```rust
fn check_command(cmd: &str) -> CheckResult {
    // Quick reject (existing)
    if global_quick_reject(cmd) {
        return CheckResult::allowed();
    }

    // NEW: Check if dangerous patterns are in safe context
    if is_safe_string_context(cmd) {
        return CheckResult::allowed();
    }

    // Continue with heredoc detection and pattern matching...
}
```

## Test Cases

### Must ALLOW (false positive prevention)
- `bd create --description="Pattern blocks rm -rf commands"`
- `git commit -m "Fix git reset --hard detection"`
- `grep "rm -rf" src/patterns.rs`
- `echo "example: git push --force"`
- `rg -e "DROP TABLE" --type sql`

### Must BLOCK (true positives preserved)
- `rm -rf /tmp/*`
- `git reset --hard HEAD~5`
- `bash -c "rm -rf /"`
- `echo "hi" | bash -c "rm -rf /"`

## Performance

- < 100 microseconds additional overhead
- Simple string prefix matching for command detection
- No regex needed for this layer

## Dependencies

- Depends on: False positive design (2cu)

## Acceptance Criteria

- The documented false-positive examples are allowed in hook mode and `dcg test`.
- True-positive executions are still blocked, including destructive strings inside:
  - `bash -c ...`
  - `sh -c ...`
  - `node -e ...` (where the code executes)
  - command substitution (`$(...)` / backticks)
- Context classification is conservative:
  - only skips matching inside explicitly-registered safe string arguments
  - does not treat unknown flags/commands as safe
- Performance overhead is bounded (target < 100µs additional overhead on typical allow-path commands).
","notes":"## Objective

Implement the execution-context + safe-string-arg behavior described in `git_safety_guard-t8x` so dcg stops blocking documentation/testing workflows (commit messages, bd descriptions, grep/rg patterns) while still blocking real executions.

## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

(Kept here so the task remains self-contained even though the description starts with Problem/Solution.)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PurpleRobin","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:00:00-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T01:52:22.183030324-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T01:52:22.183030324-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented safe-string-arg sanitizer + integrated into hook/evaluator; added unit/E2E regression coverage","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-fpim","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-2cu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T19:28:32.226122941-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-fpim","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-t8x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:02:22.808621071-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-fpim","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-t8x.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:03:42.899745326-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-fpim","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-t8x.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:03:47.932828938-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-fpim","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-99e.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:03:52.966413813-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-fu8","title":"Implement docker-compose extractor","description":"## Objective

Implement an extractor for docker-compose files to detect executable command contexts.

## File Pattern

- `docker-compose.yml`
- `docker-compose.yaml`
- `compose.yml`
- `compose.yaml`

## Extraction Targets

### Command Blocks (executed)
- `command:` values
- `entrypoint:` values
- `healthcheck.test:` commands

### NOT Extracted (data only)
- `environment:` values
- `labels:` values
- Volume paths
- Comments

## Example

```yaml
services:
  app:
    command: /bin/sh -c "rm -rf /data/*"  # EXTRACT THIS
    environment:
      CLEANUP: "rm -rf /"  # DO NOT extract
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- Correctly extracts command/entrypoint with line numbers
- Handles both string and array formats
- Ignores environment variables and labels
- Unit tests with representative fixtures","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### False positive controls
- Extract only from executed compose fields (e.g., `command`, `entrypoint`) and avoid scanning:
  - environment variables
  - comments
  - labels/metadata
- Include negative fixtures for dangerous strings in env/labels.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:24:57.298774964-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T23:30:32.054101743-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-fu8","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:25:20.580453194-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-fu8","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:46:02.44142759-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
